category_title: Engaged elementary students with mild disabilities or general education needs
student_description: This student is highly engaged and eager to share personal experiences but struggles with abstract vocabulary and becomes easily overwhelmed or aggressive when discussing emotionally charged topics like bullying. They rely heavily on provided templates rather than generating original work unless given structured brainstorming support. They uniquely require psychological distance (e.g., using animal characters) and extended discussion time to safely process volatile issues.

criteria.rationale: Because the student strongly prefers discussing issues over completing creative tasks, relying on a single delivery method will lead to task avoidance over time.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan incorporates multiple lesson delivery strategies, explicitly balancing verbal discussion time with structured creative tasks.

criteria.rationale: The student has high engagement and a strong need to share personal experiences, which requires more processing time than standard pacing allows.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan schedules extended time blocks (e.g., doubling standard lesson time) specifically to allow the student to fully share and process personal experiences without being cut short.
